items.title: Afghanistan says 400 killed in Kabul hospital attack as Pakistan denies responsibility
items.summary: Afghan authorities allege a drug-user rehabilitation hospital was hit amid an escalating conflict with its neighbour, Pakistan.

items.title: Woolworths may close 'one of its most remote' stores. Locals fear their town won't survive
items.summary: The closure of Rio Tinto's bauxite mine will be tough enough for this Arnhem Land town, but now Woolworths may pull out too.

items.title: Taiwanese Indigenous dance at Women's Asian Cup briefly cancelled, politician claims
items.summary: A Taiwanese Indigenous dance performance was reinstated before the Taiwan-China football quarter final on Saturday, after diplomats intervened.

items.title: Government reverses SBS Western Sydney expansion decision
items.summary: The government says it will continue to back the "important work" of SBS after a decision the broadcaster described as disappointing.

items.title: Consumer watchdog summoning major fuel suppliers to explain skyrocketing prices
items.summary: Labor has previously asked the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission to monitor prices after war erupted to ensure motorists weren't taken for "mugs".

items.title: The RBA faces its 'most complicated' rates call in years. What could it mean for you?
items.summary: The Reserve Bank was until recently expected to keep rates on hold at today's meeting. Now economists aren't so sure.

items.title: Remaining Iranian footballers join A-League team for training session as teammates depart
items.summary: Five of the seven squad members who sought asylum in Australia and reversed their decisions have rejoined the team in Kuala Lumpur.

items.title: Australia not among US allies asked to send navy vessels to the Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Defence Minister Richard Marles says the government would consider any request to participate in the conflict, but none had yet arrived.

items.title: Israeli ground offensive in Lebanon 'must be averted', countries say in joint statement
items.summary: Leaders of Canada, France, Germany, Italy and the UK have warned of "devastating consequences" as Israel says it's conducting a ground operation.

items.title: How to make SBS News your preferred source on Google
items.summary: Google now allows you to pick your preferred sources in Top Stories.

items.title: Does a 'Great Firewall' loom? Why Russians are buying up walkie-talkies and road maps
items.summary: Some believe Russia is testing a system that could cut it off from the global internet.

items.title: Sentimental Value just won an Oscar. Meet the skateboard-smuggling director behind it
items.summary: Joachim Trier reflects on his family, punk roots, messy lives and the quiet moments that inspire his films.

items.title: Fresh warning over risky financial advice targeting young Australians
items.summary: A survey commissioned by the country's financial watchdog suggests nearly two thirds of gen Z trust financial information on social media.

items.title: Fifth member of Iranian football team leaves Australia after withdrawing asylum bid
items.summary: Team captain Zahra Ghanbari has withdrawn her asylum bid, after three other members of the team left Australia over the weekend.

items.title: Iran denies Trump's claim of seeking ceasefire as it warns countries against wider war
items.summary: Countries have responded cautiously to the US president's call to send ships to the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: How is watching the war in the Middle East impacting our mental health?
items.summary: The recent conflict in Iran and across the Middle East is exposing audiences to atrocities and human suffering on a large scale. Here's how people can stay informed, without becoming overwhelmingly distressed.

items.title: For 200 years, this community has called an Australian island home. Now its future is in doubt
items.summary: In one of Australia's most remote places, a unique community navigates rising seas and fears of relocation and cultural loss.

items.title: Max lives in a 'renters' utopia', but some say the city's famous housing system isn't fair
items.summary: As rental affordability hits a new low in Australia, a European city boasts a decades-long social housing model which houses about 50 per cent of its population.

items.title: Recession not expected in Australia but markets suggest a March rate hike: Treasurer
items.summary: Traders and economists widely expect the Reserve Bank to lift interest rates but uncertainty over the Iran war could convince the board to keep them on hold.

items.title: Danish royals Queen Mary and King Frederik begin Australia visit with Uluru trek
items.summary: Denmark's monarchs have followed in the footsteps of the late Princess Diana and now-King Charles with a visit to a site in Australia's red centre.

items.title: Trump urges other nations to help secure shipping through Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Iran had threatened that US-linked oil and energy firms would be "turned into a pile of ashes" if they were hit.

items.title: Amid rationing and shortage fears, here's what Australia's fuel supply looks like in charts
items.summary: Energy Minister Chris Bowen has released 20 per cent of the nation's fuel reserves as experts warn rationing could be on the cards.

items.title: How war in the Middle East is 're-ordering power' and what it means for Australia's alliances
items.summary: The war in the Middle East has signalled changes to the international order, with some saying the system is in "severe crisis".

items.title: Energy minister urges calm over fuel stocks as consumer watchdog scrutinises price increases
items.summary: The government has assured Australians that there is no fuel supply shortage, as the New South Wales government convenes a fuel security roundtable.
